## 0. POST-DECISION + FULL DATE-JOIN STATE (D1-D9, 2026-05-31 — see overrides/decisions.md)

**Review queue: 553 → 218.** **Parked (S02, staged, NOT imported): 290.** Conservation PASS on every entity (episodes 74=74, distributions 190=190, episode_guests 173=173 incl. 9 folded).

| entity | import rows | needs_human | parked S02 |
|---|---|---|---|
| episodes | 19 (S03) | 3 | 55 |
| distributions | 44 | 24 | 146 |
| guests | 181 | 104 | — |
| episode_guests | 8 | 67 | 89 |
| premade_library | 380 | 20 | — |

### Production → air_date join (the correction — air_dates were in the schedule)
Joined every production episode to the GSN schedule by **(month + show-slot = week)**, NOT by broadcast-EP
(which has the dupes: Mar EP005×2, malformed EP09). **16 of 19 spine episodes now carry a confirmed
air_date** (E001-004, E006-009, E011, E016-018, E021-024). **0 ambiguous slots.** The 3 overflows
(E005/E010/E025 = Show-5 of 4-week months) stay needs_human — **no invented dates** (this reverses D9;
E025 is blank, not 2026-06-02).

### Platform bridge (now that episodes are dated)
- **Distributions: 20 attached** via 2 agreeing signals (platform's S03E0NN + consistent publish-vs-air
  date-lag, ~34d). 12 bridge-unresolved + 12 unclassified (blank uid) stay needs_human. *(Caveat: the two
  signals share the number assumption — confirmed by lag-consistency, not an independent guest/title key.)*
- **Appearances: 9 folded** into clean episode_guests (5 schedule exact-match D2 + 4 youtube guest-name).

### Decisions
- **Import-ready S03: 16 dated episodes** + 8 scripted interview guests (5 now 2-3-source).
- **D6 FINAL:** `do_not_contact=false` on all 7 (jeanson, purdom, wile, carter, jeffrey-tomkins, bell-verle, carson-ben); bogus "deceased" flag cleared. **Real deceased/DNC list is UNVERIFIED — the source section was mis-parsed — TODO next session.**
- **D7:** merged `joeseph-hubbard`+`joe-hubbard`→`joseph-hubbard`, `tomkins-jeff`+`jeff-tomkins`→`jeffrey-tomkins`. **D4:** 14 new youtube creates + 6 fuzzy merge-candidates.
- **D8:** 52 May tracker rows remapped E016→E021 (E021 now rc|scripts|tracker = 3-source); 17 April E016 kept.

Detailed sections below = pre-decision baseline + methodology (audit trail).

## 1. Deliverables — row counts (pre-decision baseline)

| entity | rows | ≥2-source | conflicts | needs_human |

| distributions | 190 | 0 | 0 | 190 |
| guests | 167 | 29 | 0 | 108 |

| premade_library | 380 | 0 | 0 | 20 |

**TRUE review queue (grand total): 553**
(distributions 190 · episode_guests 165 · guests 108 · episodes 70 · premade 20)
- premade's 20 are flagged in-table (overlay, no separate file) — included here for the true count.
- guests 108 **includes the 24 youtube-only** identities (+ 84 contact-sourced); 108 rows = **100 distinct guests** (8 appear twice = both PII-review and flagged).

> guests **≥2-source = 29**: the 3 contact-sheet exports count as ONE logical source;
> a guest is multi-source only if it ALSO appears in youtube-guests or an appearance record.
> The other 138 are contact-only (`medium`, not `high`). `contact_export_copies` column tracks
> how many of the 3 exports each guest came from.

> Sweep 1 baseline is intentionally large. It shrinks as decisions land in `overrides/`
> (empty this sweep). The README convergence target (near-empty needs_human) is sweep ~3.

## 1a. Conservation — no collected record dropped

Every collected source row lands in either its table or a needs_human file. Verified per entity:

| entity | table | needs_human | collected | accounted | conserve |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| episode_guests | 8 | 165 | 173 | 173 | ✅ PASS |
| distributions | 190 | 190 | 190 | 190 | ✅ PASS |
| guests | 167 | 108 | 191 | 191 | ✅ PASS |
| episodes | 74 | 70 | 74 | 74 | ✅ PASS |
| premade_library | 380 | — | 380 | 380 | ✅ PASS |

Two accounting models (don't conflate them):
- **Bucket** (`episode_guests`, youtube-only guests): table = clean, needs_human = separate pile → `table + needs_human = collected`. episode_guests: 8 + 165 = 173 (scripts 10 + schedule 51 + youtube 112).
- **Overlay** (`distributions`, `episodes`): table holds ALL rows; needs_human is a flag-list pointing at a subset of those same rows → `collected = table`, needs_human ⊆ table.

**Fixed this revision:** `appearances_youtube.csv` (112) was never loaded by `build_episode_guests` and 2 scripts run-of-show rows were skipped — 114 appearances silently dropped. Now every appearance is routed; `reconcile.py` asserts `collected == emitted` (conservation_ok=True).

## 2. Episode spine

- **19 canonical S03** (`key_status=canonical`, from RC + scripts + tracker).
- **55 provisional S02** (`key_status=provisional`, platform numbering, **every row needs_human=true** — see §6).
- **4 rows are import-clean with real air_dates**: **S03E021–E024** (`rc|scripts`, high confidence,
  needs_human=false). **These unblock the cloud guest-cooldown spacing pass.**
- S03E025 (May Show 5) = high-confidence episode but air_date **blank + needs_human** (overflow past
  the 4 May broadcast weeks — no clean schedule row).
- S03E001–E020: episode_uid solid from production sources, but **air_date blank + needs_human**
  (no confident production→broadcast bridge; not guessed).

---

## 2b. episode_guests routing — why "8 clean" is a VERIFIED outcome

**One-line reason (split — NOT uniformly "nothing to attach"):** the 112 youtube appearances are
needs_human because **0** reference a canonical-clean episode (all S02-provisional or S03 youtube-publish
numbers ≠ E021-024) — nothing to attach, confirmed; the 51 schedule appearances are mixed — 43 reference
non-canonical episodes (nothing to attach) but **8 DO reference canonical-clean E021-024** (5 exact
guest+uid corroborations + billy-hallowell/E021 + 2 blank-key) and were routed to needs_human only
because the air_date+title join is **wrong-keyed for the schedule** — i.e. for those 8 it IS a
too-strict join, not absence of a match.

"8 clean / 165 needs_human" is structurally forced, not a coincidence:

- **Scripts is the ONLY appearance source that carries the production episode number on the
  appearance row** (season+episode → S03E021-025). Those 8 attach to canonical episodes directly,
  no join required → clean table.
- **`appearances_schedule.csv` and `appearances_youtube.csv` carry NO air_date column, NO title
  column, and zero rows referencing any 2026-05 date** (verified). The canonical episodes have **no
  title** (only an RC slot label). So the authorized **air_date+title join has no keys to fire on**
  for any of the 163 platform/schedule appearances → all correctly land in needs_human. Missing join
  keys, not over-strict matching.

**Known imperfection (promotion candidate — NOT auto-applied, awaiting Daniel's ruling):**
`appearances_schedule.csv` is internally **dual-numbered** — it contains both an old monthly-interview
sequence (e.g. tim-clarey=S03E001) AND production numbers. **5 schedule rows exactly corroborate the
scripts canonical mapping on BOTH production episode_uid AND guest_key**: legates=E021, clarey=E022,
janzen=E023, werner=E024, wang=E024. They are currently flagged "numbering ≠ production" in
`episode_guests.needs_human.csv`, but the numbering DOES match production there — so they are real
2-source confirmations mis-filed as unresolved.

**Recommended fix (pending go):** add a conservative corroboration join — attach a schedule/youtube
appearance to a canonical episode ONLY when `episode_uid` is a canonical production uid AND `guest_key`
matches the scripts mapping for that episode. Effect: promotes those 5 episode_guests rows from
source_count=1 → 2 and moves 5 rows out of needs_human. Exact agreement only (not fuzzy), so it is
corroboration, not an auto-fix of ambiguous data.

---

## 3. Three-scheme cross-walk (the numbering ruling, applied)

Canonical `episode_uid` = **production** number only (RC + scripts + tracker agree). Every platform's
own number is stored as `platform_episode_number` (+ `platform_numbering_scheme`) on its distributions
row — never used to set `episode_uid`.

| production (canonical) | air_date | gsn_broadcast | fireside | youtube |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S03E021 (May Show 1) | 2026-05-05 | EP013 | — (no bridge) | — (no bridge) |
| S03E022 (May Show 2) | 2026-05-12 | EP014 | — | — |
| S03E023 (May Show 3) | 2026-05-19 | EP015 | — | — |
| S03E024 (May Show 4) | 2026-05-26 | EP016 | — | — |
| S03E025 (May Show 5) | **blank (needs_human)** | EP017? (6/2, ambiguous) | — | — |

**Scheme definitions**
- **production** — RC rundown titles ("May Show 1 \| S03_Ep021") + script filenames + tracker. 5 shows/month from January → May = E021-025. Canonical.
- **gsn_broadcast** — GSN airing schedule, one per week from Feb 3. May = EP013-016. Real per-platform numbering, **not an error**.
- **fireside** — Fireside's own sequential Ep 1-13. Real per-platform numbering.
- **youtube** — YouTube publish-order self-numbering (S02E001-053, S03E001-013). Provisional only.

`—` = no air_date/title bridge exists this sweep to map that platform's number to the production
episode. Those platform rows sit in `distributions.needs_human.csv` pending a title/date bridge.

## 4. Conflicts logged (not silently resolved)

- **Tracker May mislabel** (`episodes.conflicts.csv`): the graphics tracker keys the May batch as
  **S03E016**, while RC + scripts say May = **S03E021-025**. Logged, **not merged**. The tracker's
  May graphics rows are mis-keyed and need a human remap to E021-025.
- Guest export disagreements: **0** (`guests.conflicts.csv` empty — the 3 contact-list exports agree
  on every field for all 167 guests).

## 5. Audit pass (3 read-only agents — report-only, single-writer = LEAD)

| agent | result |
|---|---|
| **AUDIT-KEYS** | **0 discrepancies.** Every S03 uid re-derived from RC/scripts/tracker; `sources` column accurate; all 55 S02 = provisional+needs_human; tracker E016 conflict present; no orphans. |
| **AUDIT-DATES** | **0 discrepancies.** Every non-blank air_date backed by a real GSN schedule row; E021-024 → 5/5-5/26 confirmed; E025 overflow blank+needs_human; no S02 has a date. |
| **AUDIT-GUESTS** | **Sound.** No dropped keys (all 167 accounted for), no false merges, denylist (21) quarantined, variant pairs flagged not merged. One labeling nit found → **fixed** (below). |

**Audit-driven fix applied:** `malformed_email` (guest `danny-faulkner`) was in `conflict_fields` but
not in `conflicts.csv`. Split data-quality flags into a new `quality_flags` column so
`conflict_fields` ⟺ `conflicts.csv` stays an invariant. No data auto-fixed — guest still needs_human.

**Name-variant pairs** (kept SEPARATE, all in `guests.needs_human.csv` for your ruling — never auto-merged):
`joe-hubbard`/`joseph-hubbard` · `michael-houts`/`michael-houtz` · `jeff-tomkins`/`jeffrey-tomkins` ·
`john-mackay`/`john-mckay`.

## 6. Carryover needs-human flags (domain, not auto-fixable)

- **THD voice gap** — no authored THD scripts exist; only YouTube ASR (auto-caption) transcripts, which
  carry mis-transcriptions ("I'm David Reeves", "coal"→"cool/cold/Cole") and no punctuation. Cannot be
  stored as verbatim David voice. (`voice/kill_list.md` K4.) Needs Daniel + authored source.
- **Monologue reuse** — **March Show 2 monologue is byte-identical to January Show 2** (Saturn's Hexagon,
  content hash `ae923ac24e11`). (`voice/kill_list.md` K3.) Flag, don't dedupe blindly.
- **Guest-list quality** — ~20 name-variant/swaps + 1 malformed email + a do-not-contact/Featured-Resource/
  deceased section (21 rows). All quarantined in `guests.needs_human.csv`; none leaked to clean `guests.csv`.
- **GSN on-demand catalog** — `distributions_gsn_ondemand.csv` = **0 rows is CORRECT** (delivery portal,
  not a public catalog). Not a gap.

---

## 7. S02 is platform-provisional (read before importing)

S02 has **no production source** (no RC/scripts/tracker coverage). Its 55 episode_uids are keyed
**provisionally from platform numbering** (YouTube/Fireside self-numbering), `key_status=provisional`,
`needs_human=true` on every row. **Downstream must never treat an S02 uid as authoritative.** A future
production-source sweep (or Daniel's S02 numbering ruling) is required to make S02 canonical.

---

- Import-ready **now**: the 4 dated canonical episodes **S03E021-024** + their 8 scripted interview
  guests (`episode_guests.csv`) + 167 reconciled guests (29 cross-source-confirmed, 138 contact-only
  `medium`; PII held in review file).
- Everything else is staged + honestly flagged for the `overrides/` review loop.
